spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates autocorrect='on' vs 'off' recommendations for form fields using type, name, id, autocomplete, and spellcheck signals.

interactive.rationale: Audit, apply recommendation, and reset controls update the form, report, summary counters, and recommended HTML.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index blurb matches the scan, flag, and one-click rewrite flow.

edge_cases.rationale: The audit heuristic now has an explicit prose/display allowlist for display names, full names, bios, descriptions, and profile summaries before applying technical-field patterns.

references.rationale: The see-also block includes the canonical chromestatus.com/feature/6264645053710336 link.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, with button colours and contrast aligned to the shared design system.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: Scan + flag + one-click HTML rewrite based on type/name/id/autocomplete heuristics, now with an explicit prose allowlist so display names, full names, bios, and profile summaries stay autocorrect-on. The page embeds the canonical ChromeStatus link. Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp: display name and bio stayed on, username/email/OTP/snippet switched off, allowlist rationale appeared, and console/network were clean.
